subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear headings and sub-headings with a distinct introduction, body and conclusion.
Tables and graphs are positioned close to the discussion that references them.
The aim is stated clearly and succinctly, anchoring the investigation.
weaknesses: Extended narrative in rationale and repeated explanations add unnecessary length.
Occasional digressions (personal anecdotes, repeated citation lists) reduce conciseness.
Minor coherence lapsesâlinks between sections are sometimes abrupt.
Reference entries are duplicated and inconsistently styled; broken footnote indicators hinder readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments (organization 3, conciseness 2, coherence 2) average around band 2â3. The work is well organized but contains enough unnecessary material and minor logical jumps to warrant a clear mid-band award of 2 out of 4.

strengths: Formula for TS% is presented correctly with variables clearly defined.
Interpretation of rÂ² as percentage of variance explained is well articulated.
Explanation of p-value meaning is clear and correctly outlines its role in hypothesis testing.
weaknesses: Only a single scatter plot is interpreted; additional representations (e.g., residual plot) are missing.
Notation is inconsistent (mixing % and decimal, mis-subscripted symbols).
Logical clarity is unevenâmisapplication of a two-sample t-test to assess correlation.
At times the text implies causation rather than correlation, which is misleading.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands were placed in band 2. There is correct use of some representations and notation but with inconsistencies and uneven clarity, so a band-2 score of 2 out of 4 is appropriate.

strengths: The rationale links the investigation to the studentâs own interests (height, viewing experience).
Use of a self-selected NBA data set shows personal motivation.
Linking conclusions to personal practice demonstrates engagement.
weaknesses: Independent thinking is minimal beyond standard textbook procedures.
The statistical approach follows a generic methodology with little adaptation.
Creativity and initiative are lowâno novel data treatment or alternative models explored.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three personal-engagement strands were placed in the lowest non-zero band. The work shows some personal voice but limited independent thinking or creative initiative, meriting 1 out of 3.

strengths: Acknowledges rounding limitations and that correlation does not imply causation.
Brief evaluation of sample-size limitations is present.
Recognizes at least some methodological constraints.
weaknesses: Reflection is descriptive rather than probing; lacks depth.
No discussion of how conclusions might change or of future investigative steps.
Critical analysis is superficial and does not address broader validity or confounding factors.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each reflection strand was placed in band 1: limited and basic evaluation without substantial critical insight or future implications. A score of 1 out of 3 is therefore justified.

strengths: Correct computation of Pearsonâs r and rÂ², demonstrating relevant mathematical understanding.
Correlation and significance testing are appropriate to the aim and apply syllabus content.
Mathematics used is commensurate with course level and supports the investigation well.
weaknesses: The t-test formula is misapplied (used two-sample test instead of correlation-based test).
Inconsistencies in numeric values for r between table and text.
Degrees of freedom and critical-value discussion are missing, and hypotheses are phrased too broadly.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use-of-mathematics strands were assessed at bands 2, 3 and 3. The work uses appropriate syllabus mathematics effectively but contains conceptual errors and minor accuracy issues. A mid-upper band award of 3 out of 6 accurately reflects this performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanatory paragraphs accompany calculations, yet logical flow weakens where incorrect reasoning is introduced (t-test on means to justify significance of r).  Consequently clarity is uneven, suiting the middle band descriptor of âsome logical structure and clarity.â

The student successfully computes r and rÂ², but the t-test contains conceptual errors and the explanatory text occasionally misinterprets statistical meaning.  These issues show only some understanding with noticeable errors, best fitting the 2-mark band.

A scatter plot with trend line, a large calculation table and algebraic formulae provide more than one representation, but only the scatter plot is interpreted in any depth and no additional visuals (e.g., residual plot) are offered.  Hence the work shows âsome useâ rather than effective use of multiple representations.

The exploration follows a recognisable progression â introduction, methodology, calculations, evaluation â but the argument occasionally stalls (e.g., the t-test section does not logically stem from the correlation study and contains conceptual flaws).  Links between sections are present yet sometimes abrupt, so the reader must infer parts of the chain of reasoning.  Overall, the work displays some but not full coherence, best matching the mid-band description.

The IA applies standard statistical tools without modification and does not venture into creative data treatment or alternative models.  Initiative is therefore minimal and fits the lowest achievement band that is above zero.

The student states that the t-test result is âextremely significantâ and that correlation does not imply causation, but provides little deeper examination of why the statistical choices may be flawed or what the numbers truly imply.  The analysis is basic.

Outcome evaluation notes sample-size limitations and suggests adding median players, but does not discuss how conclusions might alter or propose future investigative steps.  The evaluation therefore remains limited.
Reflection is confined to one brief evaluation section acknowledging rounding and correlation-causation; it is descriptive rather than probing.  This constitutes only limited reflection.

Key formulae are typeset and variables defined, but notation is sometimes inconsistent (e.g., mixing % and decimal, mis-subscripted xÌ).  The t-test formula given is for independent samples, not for testing a correlation, which shows partial rather than consistent control of mathematical language.

A short rationale links the study to the studentâs height and viewing experience, yet the remainder of the exploration is detached and generic.  The personal voice is present but not strongly embedded in the mathematical decisions, matching the lower band.

The text is generally to the point, yet extended narrative in the rationale and repeated explanations of well-known tests add unnecessary length.  Occasional digressions (personal anecdotes, repeated citation lists) reduce conciseness, so the strand aligns with âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements.â

Correlation, rÂ² and significance testing are all relevant to the aim and are applied in context.  However, only a subset of available data is used, and the significance test is misapplied; still, the mathematics chosen is appropriate and supports the investigation, aligning with the 3-mark descriptor of ârelevant mathematics used appropriately.â

The choice of a self-selected NBA data set and the decision to test correlation do show the studentâs own interest, but the statistical approach follows textbook procedures with little adaptation or novel insight.  Evidence of independent thinking is therefore limited.

Headings, sub-headings, and a clear introduction/body/conclusion make the structure easy to follow.  Tables and graphs are placed close to the discussion that references them.  Minor organisational weaknesses (e.g., references embedded inside paragraphs, broken footnote indicators) stop it from being exemplary but it is still well-organised overall.

Pearson correlation and linear regression are squarely within the SL syllabus and are carried out at an expected level of sophistication.  No higher-level material is introduced, so the work is âcommensurate with course levelâ but not beyond, awarding 3 marks for this strand.

annotations.comment: The evaluation summarises steps but lacks depth on why methodological choices affect validity; incorporate critical reflection on sample selection and assumptions.
Limitation discussion focuses solely on rounding; broaden to discuss sample bias, external validity, and potential confounders.
Linking conclusions to personal action shows clear engagement and initiative to apply findings to practice.
Including median-ranking players is a good suggestion; detail how their inclusion would change variance or correlation calculations.
Reference entries are duplicated and styled inconsistently; format alphabetically and adhere to a single citation style throughout.
The title and subtitle are clear and visually balanced, effectively setting the context for the investigation.
The rationale vividly shows personal motivation and interest in the topic, anchoring the investigation in genuine experience.
The anecdotal detail in the rationale, while engaging, distracts from the aim; focus on directly relevant context to improve conciseness.
The introduction to 'Data processing' combines method description and rounding notes; separate these into distinct statements or subsections for clarity.
The t-value calculation uses sample means and deviations incorrectly; revise to use the appropriate correlation-based t formula.
The personal code field is left blank; include your personal code to meet IB submission requirements consistently.
The aim is stated clearly and succinctly, making the investigation focus immediately apparent.
The formula for TS% is presented correctly with variables defined, aiding clear mathematical communication.
The TS% formula citation relies on an undated web post; consider referencing a recognized statistical textbook for stronger credibility.
The jump from defining TS% to selecting the top and bottom players lacks a clear connective explanation; add a brief rationale to improve logical flow.
The scatter plot effectively visualizes the correlation, but adding a residual plot would strengthen the assessment of linearity assumptions.
Broken footnote encoding ('<sup>&</sup>lt;sup>8') hinders readability; correct the HTML so references display properly.
In the Pearson formula, the subscript on the second summation term is missing; align notation with $$r=\frac{\sum(x-\bar{x})(y-\bar{y})}{\sqrt{\sum(x-\bar{x})^2\sum(y-\bar{y})^2}}$$.
The inline definitions of x and y repeat the same symbol twice; clarify each to avoid confusion (e.g., use distinct notation for means and deviations).
Presenting the full calculation table demonstrates thoroughness, but consider moving it to an appendix and summarizing key results here.
Relying solely on an online tool for computations saves time, but manually working through a sample row would deepen understanding and engagement.
The summed value used for r in the equation (13.6432) conflicts with the tableâs total (13.6437); reconcile these discrepancies to maintain accuracy.
Mentioning degrees of freedom and the critical t-value when introducing the significance test would complete the logical argument.
The text states âvariables have a causationâ, which is misleading; revise to emphasize that correlation does not imply causation.
Interpreting rÂ² as the percentage of variance explained is well articulated and adds depth to the analysis.
The twoâsample t-test formula is unsuitable for correlation significance; employ $$t = r\sqrt{\frac{n-2}{1-r^2}}$$ for testing Pearsonâs r.
Hypotheses are stated broadly; rephrase Hâ and Hâ to reference the population correlation coefficient (e.g., Ï=0 vs Ïâ 0).
The explanation of p-value meaning is clear and correctly outlines its role in hypothesis testing.
Reporting p<0.0001 is precise; for completeness, include degrees of freedom alongside the significance level.
